verbatim_text: And just to remind you, you know, it's simple because the test problem is solvable once you understand the relevant data. So really, the data is the focus here, right? And the goal is to efficiently process a lot of relevant data given at test time. Now, machine learning has a special word for relevant data given at test time. You know, at training time, you just call it data. At test time, we call it context. Okay? And when there's a lot of context, we call it long context. It's just a special name for a lot of relevant data given at test time. And the long context problem is very valuable. In a lot of applications, you can have, you know, say a large legal case, you know, a large legal case, and you want to put out the talking points, you want to find bugs in higher code bases or you have, you know, many histories with interactions with users. And people try very hard on this problem because, you know, it's very valuable, especially for enterprises. In fact, you know, there's basically a consensus in industry that long context is one of the most economically valuable problems in machine learning today and one of the biggest bottlenecks. So how does it become a bottleneck?
